title: practical english usage - Part 283
content:
due -+ duly true -+ truly argue -+ argument Exceptions: words ending in -ue definite -+ definitely excite -+ excitement complete -+ completeness Before endings that begin with a consonant, final -e is not normally dropped.

2 final    -e  not   dropped       before     consonants agree -+ agreeable dye -+ dyeing see -+ seeing     canoe -+ canoeist Final -e is not dropped from words ending in -ee, -oe or -ye.

mileage (only with e) mov(e)able (both forms common) likeable (usually with e) lhe form without -e is more common in most cases. Note: Some words that end in -e have two possible forms before -able and -age.

An exception in British English is ageing (more common than aging).

make -+ making fame -+ famous hope -+ hoping note -+ notable shade -+ shady to a word that ends in -e, we  usually drop the -e.

When an ending that begins with a vowel (e.g. -ing, -able, -ous) is added 1 final    -e  dropped       before     vowels 346 final    e Word  Formation and SpellingSection 30 vocabulary •  347   doubling final consonants picnic -> picnickers panic -> panicking mimic -> mimicked Final -c changes to ck before -ed, -er, -ing, etc.

Final -s is sometimes doubled in 'focus(s)ing and 'focus(s)ed.

combat -> 'combating'  combatting worship -> worshippers (AmE also 'worshipers) kidnap ->  'kidnapped handicap ->  'handicapped main stress.

pronounced with full vowels (e.g. /æ/),   even when these do not carry the Consonants are sometimes doubled at the end of final syllables that are 7 other     exceptions In American English, words like this are most often spelt with one /: traveling.

'travel -> 'travelling equal ->  equalled in most cases, even in unstressed syllables.

In British English, we  double -l at the end of a word after one vowel letter final  I inunstressed syllables 6 exception: develop -> developing -> developed (NOT   developping, developped) not gallop -> 'galloping -> galloped (    gallopping, gallopped) Note the spelling of these words: bu refer -> referring  T   offer -> 'offering but begin ->  beginning           'open -> 'opening butupset -> upsetting            'visit ->  'visiting words that end in unstressed syllables. Compare: We   only double consonants in stressed syllables. We   do not double in longer 5  only    stressed      syllables but                                       not bet -> betting  beat -> beating (     beatting) but                                not fat ->  fatter              fast -> faster (     fastter) Compare: We   only double when the word ends in one consonant after one vowel letter.

4 one   consonant        after    one   vowel     letter but plan ->  planned   phone ->  phoned but fat -> fatter       late ->  later but hop -> hopping   hope -> hoping We   only double consonants that come at the end of a word. Compare: 3 only    at  the   end   of  a  word not show -> showing  flow -> flowed (     showwing, flowwed) Final w (in words like show, flow) is part of a vowel sound, and is not doubled.

for ‘ questionable’ , ‘ uncertain’ ).

final -z in quizzes, fezzes, and final -f in iffy (a colloquial word We   double final -s in gassing, gassed (but not usually in other words), vocabulary •  349   ch and tch, k and ck catch fetch stitch botch hutch back neck sick   lock   stuck sounds /k/  and /tʃ/.

After one vowel, at the end of a word, we  usually write -ck and -tch for the 349 ch   and   tch,    k  and   ck but die -> dying lie lying (  dye -> dyeing) We   change -ie to -y- before -ing.

5 changing       /e  to  y say -> said pay -» paid lay -» laid Exceptions: enjoy enjoyment grey -» greyish buy -» buying    play -> played We   do not change -y to -i- after a vowel letter.

4 no   change      after    a  vowel try -» trying lory -> Toryism baby -» babyish We   do not change -y to -i- before i (for example when we  add -ing, -ism, -ish).

3 no   change      before     / Words formed from the adjective sly: slyer, slyest, slyly, slyness.

Words formed from the adjective dry: normally drier, driest, dryly/drily, dryness.
